meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. An intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: To solve the risks of no version control while building SaaS pricing models, implement a Git-based architecture that tracks every pricing logic iteration. By automating deployment through Claude Code and managing state in Antigravity, you secure your codebase against regression while building a professional, subscription-ready SaaS foundation for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of code that represents your first major project, yet a cold sweat sets in. Every time you tweak your Stripe integration or adjust a pricing tier, you live in terror of overwriting your only working copy. The lack of version control isn't just a technical oversight; it is the silent killer of student innovation and professional ambition.

But here is the turning point: you are about to bridge the gap between amateur scripting and professional architecture. By integrating structured version control with a robust SaaS pricing engine, you will transform your project from a fragile script into a scalable, industry-grade application. This is where your career as a developer truly begins.

steps.title: Initialize Repository and Environment
steps.description: Establish a secure foundation. We will initialize Git and configure your environment to ensure every pricing logic change is logged, preventing the 'no version control' disaster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure directory structure for a SaaS application with a focus on version-controlled pricing modules and environment variable management.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit: Setup project structure'

steps.title: Design the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define your pricing tiers. We need a flexible schema that supports monthly and annual billing, ensuring that the logic is decoupled from the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including tier names, price_id, interval, and feature_flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing_schema.ts file with interfaces for subscription tiers'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to real-world payments. We will build a handler that listens for subscription events to update user status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Node.js express route for Stripe webhooks that handles subscription.created and subscription.deleted events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create webhook.ts to handle Stripe subscription events and save to DB'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Workflows
steps.description: Configure your deployment to run tests before pushing to production, ensuring that your pricing logic remains intact across every version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting and unit tests for the pricing module on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated testing'

steps.title: Finalize and Document
steps.description: Create a professional README that highlights how your architecture solves common version control issues in SaaS development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical README.md for a SaaS project emphasizing the robust version control and pricing implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'Finalize pricing module and documentation'

pro_tips: Always use feature flags for pricing experiments so you can toggle features without rolling back the entire codebase.
Implement Semantic Versioning (SemVer) for your API routes; your future self will thank you when you need to update pricing tiers.
Use git-hooks to automatically run a 'build' check before any commit to ensure your pricing logic isn't broken.
Separate your business logic from your UI components to make unit testing your pricing engine significantly easier.
Treat your environment variables like production keys—never hardcode your Stripe API keys, even in early development.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy your project to a platform like Vercel or Railway. Monitor your first test transaction, then add a 'Free Tier' versus 'Pro Tier' visual comparison page to your frontend to complete your professional portfolio piece.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a scalable, version-controlled SaaS product that stands out to recruiters. Do not let your code remain a mystery to your future self; take control of your architecture today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
